title: Warm Smoky Italian Pork Risotto
description: A creamy and smoky Italian dish featuring dried leaves of summer savoury, cooked in a ttukbaegi earthenware pot, perfect for a sick day, and nut-free-friendly. This recipe is a hearty and comforting meal that is sure to satisfy.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the ttukbaegi earthenware pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Add the smoked pork sausage and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the Arborio rice and cook, stirring constantly, for 1-2 minutes, until the rice is coated in oil and slightly toasted.
Add the white wine and cook, stirring constantly, until the liquid is almost completely absorbed, about 2 minutes.
Add 1/2 cup of warm chicken broth to the pot and cook, stirring constantly, until the liquid is mostly absorbed, repeating this process until all the broth is used, about 20 minutes.
Stir in the crushed summer savoury leaves, Grana Padano cheese, salt, and black pepper.
Serve the risotto hot, garnished with additional summer savoury leaves if desired.
